STEP0.TITLE: Order Creation
STEP0.WELCOME:
Welcome to Logistics Optimization System! 🎉

You will learn the system step by step with this tour.

Step 1: Order Creation
STEP0.PROFILE:
Profile Menu

You can access your profile and change your language settings here.
STEP0.SIDENAV:
Menu Navigation

You can access other menus from here.
STEP0.TEMPLATE:
Excel Template

You can download the Excel template from here.

You can upload bulk orders with the template.
STEP0.MANUAL_ENTRY:
Manual Order Entry

You can make manual selection and add your products.

Let's click!
STEP0.ADD_BUTTON:
Add Product

You can add products to your order from here.

Let's add a product!

DIALOG.PRODUCT_SEARCH:
Product Search

You can search by typing the product name here.

We will create a sample order.

STEP0.ORDER_SUMMARY:
Order Summary

You can view and edit order details from here.

  • Order date
  • Customer information
  • Vehicle selection
  • Weight limits
STEP0.ORDER_DETAILS:
Order Details

The products you added are listed here.

You can edit or delete products.
STEP0.NEXT_BUTTON:
Go to Palletization Step

Now let's move to the palletization step by clicking the Next button.

The tour will continue automatically there! 🚀

STEP1.TITLE: Palletization and Optimization
STEP1.WELCOME:
Welcome to Palletization Area! 📦

Great! You have completed your order information.

Step 2: Placing products on pallets
STEP1.CALCULATE:
Automatic Calculation

You can have the system automatically place items by clicking the Calculate button.

Genetic algorithm finds the most optimal placement! 🧬

Let's click!
STEP1.AFTER_CALCULATE:
Great! ✅

The system has automatically placed the products on pallets.

Now let's look at other features!
STEP1.PRODUCT_SEARCH:
Product Search

You can use the search box to quickly find products.
STEP1.REMAINING_PRODUCTS:
Product Inventory

All products in your order are listed in the left section.

  • You can drag products to pallets with drag&drop
  • You can split products with the split button
STEP1.PALLET_SEARCH:
Pallet Search

You can search for pallet types.
STEP1.PALLET_SECTION:
Pallet Types

Available pallet types are on the right section.

Drag pallets to package cards in the middle section.
STEP1.PACKAGE_SECTION:
Palletization Area

Package cards are created in the middle section.

Add a pallet first, then products to each card.
STEP1.PARAMS_BUTTON:
Parameterized Calculation

You can optimize with special parameters.

Let's try it!
STEP1.PARAMS_DIALOG:
Parameter Settings

You can adjust optimization parameters in this dialog.

Let's close it for now and continue.
STEP1.NEXT_REMINDER:
Palletization Completed! ✅

You can now place your products on pallets.

The next step will be truck loading and 3D visualization!

Click the Next button whenever you want.

STEP2.TITLE: Truck Loading and 3D View
STEP2.WELCOME:
Truck Loading and 3D View! 🚚

Final step! Placing your pallets in the truck.
STEP2.OPTIMIZE_BUTTON:
Start Optimization

Start optimization to automatically place your pallets in the truck.

Let's start!
STEP2.AFTER_OPTIMIZE:
Great! ✅

Optimization completed!

Your pallets have been placed in the truck.

Now let's examine the 3D view.
STEP2.THREEJS_VIEW:
3D Truck View

You can view how pallets are placed in the truck in 3D.

  • Rotate with mouse
  • Zoom with scroll
  • View from different angles
STEP2.VIEW_CONTROLS:
View Controls

You can view from different angles with these buttons:

  • Front: Front view of the truck
  • Side: Side view
  • Top: Top view
  • 3D: Isometric view
STEP2.REPORT_FILES:
Report Files

You can download optimization results in Excel format.

Reports include detailed placement plan.
STEP2.COMPLETE_SHIPMENT:
Complete Shipment

All operations completed!

You can confirm the shipment by clicking this button.

You have completed the tour! 🎉

CONSTRAINT.SIZE_TOLERANCE_HINT: The small difference allowed for two products' widths to be considered 'equal'
CONSTRAINT.DEPTH_SHORT_TOLERANCE_HINT: How much shorter an additional product can be compared to the main product
CONSTRAINT.COMBINATION_TOLERANCE_HINT: How closely two short products must match the main product when combined
CONSTRAINT.MIN_FILL_RATIO_HINT: Pallet acceptance threshold (0 = no fill check)
CONSTRAINT.CHECK_DEPTH_SYMMETRIC_HINT: Additional product can be both shorter and longer than the main product
CONSTRAINT.CHECK_WIDTH_SYMMETRIC_HINT: Products with different widths can be placed on the same pallet
CONSTRAINT.MAX_GEOMETRIC_RATIO_HINT: How many times smaller the additional product can be than the main product (e.g. 2.0 = half)
CONSTRAINT.ANCHOR_MIN_FULL_PALLETS_HINT: How many full pallets a product must fill to get its own uniform pallet
CONSTRAINT.FLAT_HEIGHT_THRESHOLD_HINT: Products thinner than this are automatically treated as side products
CONSTRAINT.SIDE_PRODUCTS_HINT: These products are always side products — stacked on top of other pallets
CONSTRAINT.MAX_PALLET_WEIGHT_HINT: Display warning only — pallets exceeding this weight are marked in red
CONSTRAINT.FORBIDDEN_PAIRS_COMING_SOON: Coming soon: product pairs that must never be placed on the same pallet
CONSTRAINT.REQUIRED_PAIRS_COMING_SOON: Coming soon: product pairs that should preferably be placed on the same pallet
CONSTRAINT.FORCE_TOP_COMING_SOON: Coming soon: products that can only be placed at the very top of a pallet
CONSTRAINT.FORCE_BOTTOM_COMING_SOON: Coming soon: products that can only be placed at the very bottom of a pallet
CONSTRAINT.LOAD_ORDER_COMING_SOON: Coming soon: you will be able to define the order in which products are loaded onto the truck
CONSTRAINT.SIZE_TOLERANCE_DETAIL: How close must two products' widths be to count as 'equal width'? Example: the half-millimetre difference between a 600 mm and a 600.5 mm product is a manufacturing deviation and can be treated as equal. This value should be kept very small — only to cover production tolerances. If you increase it, products with different widths will start being placed side by side and pallets will look irregular.
CONSTRAINT.DEPTH_SHORT_TOLERANCE_DETAIL: When adding an additional product to a pallet, how much shorter can it be than the main product? Example: main product is 2000 mm long, tolerance is 200 mm. Then products between 1800–2000 mm are added alongside the main product. It can only be shorter — not longer, as that would exceed the pallet boundary.
CONSTRAINT.COMBINATION_TOLERANCE_DETAIL: Can two short products combine to match a longer one? Example: an 800 mm + 400 mm combination can be placed next to a 1200 mm main product (total 1200, exact match). With a tolerance of 10 mm, 800 + 390 = 1190 is also accepted. Usually left at 0 (exact match) — relaxing it creates gaps on the pallet.
CONSTRAINT.MIN_FILL_RATIO_DETAIL: How full must a pallet be to be accepted? 0 = every pallet is accepted regardless of fill level. 30% = pallets that are less than 30% full are cancelled and their products are shown to the customer as 'remaining'. Setting this too high prevents products from being placed — for example, at 80% every pallet that isn't nearly full will be cancelled, leaving the user with a lot of manual work.
CONSTRAINT.CHECK_DEPTH_SYMMETRIC_DETAIL: Normally an additional product can only be shorter than the main product. Enabling this option makes it bidirectional: products that are both shorter and longer than the main product can be added to the same pallet. Useful for customers who want to group products with a small depth difference together. Note: the end of the additional product may extend beyond the pallet edge.
CONSTRAINT.DEPTH_SYMMETRIC_TOLERANCE_DETAIL: Bidirectional depth difference tolerance. If the depth difference between the main product and the additional product is below this value, they can be placed on the same pallet. Only active when the 'bidirectional' option above is enabled.
CONSTRAINT.CHECK_WIDTH_SYMMETRIC_DETAIL: Normally only products with the same width are placed on the same pallet — they sit neatly side by side. Enabling this option allows products with different widths to share a pallet. Note: empty strips will appear on the pallet. Example: a 600 mm wide product + a 500 mm wide product = 100 mm gap.
CONSTRAINT.WIDTH_SYMMETRIC_TOLERANCE_DETAIL: How large can the difference between two products' widths be? If below this value, they can share a pallet. Only active when the 'mixed width products' option above is enabled.
CONSTRAINT.MAX_GEOMETRIC_RATIO_DETAIL: How many times smaller than the main product can an additional product be? Default 2.0: the additional product can be half the length of the main product, so two fit side by side (e.g. 1200 mm main, 600 mm additional — two 600s side by side = 1200). Setting it to 3.0 also matches products one-third the size (three side by side). An exact multiple is required — not approximate.
CONSTRAINT.ANCHOR_MIN_FULL_PALLETS_DETAIL: How many full pallets must a product fill before it gets its own uniform pallet? Uniform pallet = a pallet containing only one product type, never mixed. Default 1: every product that fills one full pallet gets its own uniform pallet; remainders are combined with other products. Setting it to 2 is stricter — only products that fill two full pallets get a uniform pallet; everything else always goes to a mixed pallet.
CONSTRAINT.FLAT_HEIGHT_THRESHOLD_DETAIL: Products thinner than this threshold are automatically treated as side products. Side products are stacked on top of other pallets and do not try to form their own pallet. Typically set around 40–60 mm (thin radiators, accessories). Products manually added to the list below are always treated as side products regardless of their thickness.
CONSTRAINT.SIDE_PRODUCTS_DETAIL: Products in this list are treated as side products in all cases. The algorithm does not try to build a separate pallet for them — it places them in the empty space on top of other pallets. Suitable for thin, low-quantity, stackable products.
CONSTRAINT.MAX_PALLET_WEIGHT_DETAIL: This is for display warning only and does not affect the algorithm's decisions. Pallets exceeding this weight are highlighted in red on screen. Even if the limit is exceeded, the pallet is still created — only a visual indicator is added.
CONSTRAINT.FORBIDDEN_PAIRS_COMING_SOON_DETAIL: A future feature: a rule that ensures certain products are never placed on the same pallet. For example, to prevent a sharp-edged product from being on the same pallet as one that could easily be scratched.
CONSTRAINT.REQUIRED_PAIRS_COMING_SOON_DETAIL: A future feature: product pairs that are preferred to be shipped together. The algorithm will try to place these pairs on the same pallet whenever possible — for complete delivery.
CONSTRAINT.FORCE_TOP_COMING_SOON_DETAIL: A future feature: products that can only be placed at the very top of a pallet. For fragile products that would be crushed if something were placed on top of them.
CONSTRAINT.FORCE_BOTTOM_COMING_SOON_DETAIL: A future feature: products that must be placed only at the very bottom of a pallet. For heavy, sturdy products that should form the base.
CONSTRAINT.LOAD_ORDER_COMING_SOON_DETAIL: A future feature: truck loading order. Items to be delivered last to the customer go to the back of the truck; items to be delivered first go to the front.
CONSTRAINT.ENABLE_LOOSE_REMAINDER_PACKING: Collect Remaining Products in a Mixed Pallet
CONSTRAINT.ENABLE_LOOSE_REMAINDER_PACKING_HINT: Collects low-quantity remaining products onto a single pallet with a flexible arrangement
CONSTRAINT.ENABLE_LOOSE_REMAINDER_PACKING_DETAIL: After the main calculation is complete, collects low-quantity products that could not be placed on any pallet into a single pallet using a flexible arrangement. Upper layers can be smaller than lower ones and dimension compatibility is not enforced. The pallet may have a low fill rate — the goal is to fit remaining products onto a pallet rather than leaving them for the user.
CONSTRAINT.PALLET_WIDTH_MARGIN_HINT: Margin to be added to each pallet's width dimension (mm)
CONSTRAINT.PALLET_WIDTH_MARGIN_DETAIL: Used to leave side clearance between pallets. For example, entering 30 means a pallet with a 1000 mm width will be calculated as 1030 mm.
CONSTRAINT.PALLET_DEPTH_MARGIN_HINT: Margin to be added to each pallet's depth dimension (mm)
CONSTRAINT.PALLET_DEPTH_MARGIN_DETAIL: Used to leave front-to-back clearance between pallets. For example, entering 30 means a pallet with a 1400 mm depth will be calculated as 1430 mm.

BULK.TITLE: Bulk Algorithm Settings
BULK.INFO_ONLY_FILLED: Only the fields you fill in will be updated. Numeric fields left empty will retain each customer's current values.
BULK.TOGGLE_NOTE: The state of this toggle will be applied to all selected customers.
BULK.LEAVE_EMPTY: Leave empty if you don't want to change it
BULK.NO_CHANGES: You haven't filled in any fields, there is nothing to update
BULK.UPDATE_ERROR: An error occurred during the update
